comments:commaai/opendbc#3426.body:
I can take a first pass on the software validation side of this bounty.

Scope I can own immediately:
- inspect the current Ford CAN FD/TRON support gap in opendbc
- build a reproducible emulated validation harness for the F-150/TRON path, including CAN message generation/replay tests and safety-test coverage where possible
- compare against the active Ford lateral-control work (#3405/#3315) so any proposal builds on current Ford curvature/path-angle direction instead of duplicating it
- document exactly what is emulated vs what still needs vehicle/harness validation

I will not claim real F-150 2026 support without real route/hardware evidence. If anyone can provide a public or privately-shareable route, harness pinout notes, or SecOC message captures for a 2024+ F-150/TRON vehicle, I can wire that into the validation package and turn the emulated pass into a real reviewable port candidate.

0xjc65eth

issue:commaai/opendbc#3426.body: Let's get the new F-150s supported! Any [TRON platform](https://bluepilot.dev/2025/08/13/confirmed-tron-status-list/) car model is also eligible for this bounty. Requires a harness design (schematic), a high quality lateral port, and at least a proof-of-concept for longitudinal support. The full $10k is for a normal openpilot experience: user is ready to go after installing compatible hardware and openpilot. $2k for support similar to the 2021 Toyota Rav4 Prime, where it works if the user manually configures extra parameters.
issue:commaai/opendbc#3426.title: [$10k bounty] Ford F-150 2026 (TRON) support
